input.headline: Huge to-do list': Woman covers her house in rhinestones

raw.headline: Huge to-do list': Woman covers her house in rhinestones

winner: She covered her house in rhinestones so she could finally tick off "decorate exterior" from her to-do list—just don't ask which one is the sparkly checkbox.

candidates.text: I was shocked when I saw the front yard covered in rhinestones—turns out she was just practicing for her next project: a glitter bomb.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with 'glitter bomb' adds humor and ties back to the rhinestones; concise and specific."}

candidates.text: A woman covered her house in rhinestones, calling it her "huge to-do list." Critics say she's just *glittering* over her problems.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'to-do list' and 'glittering over problems' are clever and specific, with natural wordplay and surprise."}

candidates.text: She covered her house in rhinestones so she could finally tick off "decorate exterior" from her to-do list—just don't ask which one is the sparkly checkbox.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with the sparkly checkbox, concise and specific, funny and natural"}

candidates.text: I covered my house in rhinestones so I could stop hearing people say "huge to-do list" every time they walked by!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "Surprising twist on the headline with a clever play on words and specific scenario."}

candidates.text: Why did the woman with a to-do list cover her house in rhinestones? Because she wanted her chores to sparkle!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat playful and connects the to-do list with rhinestones, but the punchline feels a bit forced and lacks the surprise or specificity that would make it stand out in a humor competition."}
